Fig. 4: Failure cases illustrating distinct evaluation modes. (a) Low Content Relevance (CR): summary omits salient events. (b) Low Modality Coherence (MC): textual descriptions contradict visual evidence within the predicted interval.

Table 2: Evaluation of representative models on the LVSum benchmark. Higher values indicate better performance for Kendall’s τ , Spearman’s ρ, Content Relevance (CR), and Modality Coherence (MC). The Summary Length column reports the average percentage of video duration selected by each method. V=Video, T=Transcript

Fig. 2: Correlation vs. summary length for different models. Solid lines denote Kendall’s τ , dashed lines denote Spearman’s ρ.
